success_surface: Task successfully completed with correct keyword matching logic applied dynamically across all populated rows while preserving existing formatting.
effective_behaviors: Explored spreadsheet structure (headers, row counts, sample data) before implementing solution logic
Selected openpyxl over pandas when formatting preservation was required for non-target columns
Used dynamic row enumeration via ws.max_row instead of hardcoded ranges for sheet-level operations
Implemented case-insensitive partial string matching for flexible keyword detection
Handled empty/null cell values gracefully before processing to prevent errors
Verified output results by iterating through modified cells and printing matches
evidence: Agent ran exploration code to check sheet names, total rows, columns, headers, and sample data from columns D and F before writing the final solution
Chose openpyxl.load_workbook() and wb.save() approach which inherently preserves all existing formatting while allowing targeted value updates
Used for loop with range(2, max_row + 1) which adapts to any workbook size without requiring manual range specification
Applied str(cell_value).lower() and keyword.lower() comparisons enabling both case-insensitive matching and partial substring detection
Included conditional check 'if cell_d_value is None: continue' before string conversion to safely skip empty cells
Added verification section that printed all rows where 'Billing PO' was written, confirming correct count and matching logic

memory_items.title: Pre-Implementation Structure Analysis
memory_items.lesson: Always examine spreadsheet schema before coding solution
memory_items.generalization: When starting a new spreadsheet task, first inspect sheet names, row/column counts, header content, and sample cell values to understand the actual data layout rather than assuming structure from instructions.

memory_items.title: Format-Aware Tool Selection
memory_items.lesson: Choose libraries based on whether formatting must be retained
memory_items.generalization: Use openpyxl for any operation where existing cell styles, merged cells, or formulas in non-target areas must be preserved; avoid pandas.to_excel() when styling matters.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Row Enumeration Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Determine loop bounds from workbook metadata
memory_items.generalization: For sheet-level manipulations, derive iteration range from ws.max_row or detected last populated cell rather than hardcoding row numbers to ensure adaptability across different file sizes.

memory_items.title: Defensive Text Processing
memory_items.generalization: Convert cell values to lowercase strings before comparison operations to handle mixed-case data, and always verify type before calling string methods.

memory_items.title: Null-Safe Iteration Guard
memory_items.lesson: Check for missing values before transformation
memory_items.generalization: Include explicit None/empty checks at the start of each row's processing block to gracefully skip blanks without throwing exceptions.

memory_items.title: Post-Execution Verification Routine
memory_items.generalization: After writing to cells, iterate through the affected range and print summary statistics or sample results to confirm the operation matched expected outcomes.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Pre-Implementation Discovery
- Always inspect spreadsheet schema (sheet names, dimensions, headers, sample values) before writing solution code to validate assumptions against actual data.

### Adaptive Range Handling
- Derive iteration bounds from workbook metadata (ws.max_row, ws.max_column) rather than hardcoded ranges to handle varying file sizes.

### Format Preservation Priority
- When formatting in non-target columns must be retained, prefer openpyxl Workbook objects over pandas.DataFrames for read-write operations.

### Text Comparison Robustness
- Convert cell values to lowercase strings before pattern matching; include explicit None checks before string operations to prevent runtime errors.

### Output Validation
- After applying changes, iterate through the modified range and generate a verification report (counts, samples) to confirm correct application before finalizing.
